data.current_description: Deletes permanently all user events specified by the filter provided. Depending on the number of events specified by the filter, this operation could take hours or days to complete. To test a filter, use the list command first.
data.summary_suggestion: Permanently delete filtered user events from event store
data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes all user events matching the provided filter criteria from the event store. This is a long-running operation that may take hours or days depending on the volume of events to be deleted. The operation returns a long-running operation object that can be used to track progress. Note: This action is irreversible. It is recommended to test the filter using the list operation before executing the purge to ensure only intended events are deleted.
data.reasoning: Original had no summary and incomplete description. Added summary to clarify the action. Enhanced description to include: (1) what filter criteria are used, (2) that it's a long-running operation with return type, (3) irreversibility warning, and (4) recommendation to test filter first. This helps AI agents understand the operation's scope, duration, and risks.

message: Operation 'recommendationengine.projects.locations.catalogs.eventStores.userEvents.rejoin' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Original had no summary and overly complex description. Added concise summary. Restructured description to: (1) clarify it's a long-running operation, (2) explain the problem it solves (unjoined/incorrectly joined events), (3) describe the benefit (improved training/serving), and (4) specify return type. This improves AI agent interpretability by clearly stating when to use this operation and what to expect.

data.current_description: Triggers a user event rejoin operation with latest catalog data. Events will not be annotated with detailed catalog information if catalog item is missing at the time the user event is ingested, and these events are stored as unjoined events with a limited usage on training and serving. This API can be used to trigger a 'join' operation on specified events with latest version of catalog items. It can also be used to correct events joined with wrong catalog items.
data.summary_suggestion: Rejoin user events with latest catalog data
data.description_suggestion: Triggers a long-running operation to rejoin user events with the latest catalog data. This operation enriches previously unjoined events (those missing catalog item details at ingestion time) or corrects events joined with outdated/incorrect catalog items. Rejoined events will have complete catalog information for improved training and serving performance. Returns a long-running operation object that can be monitored for completion status.
data.reasoning: Original had no summary and overly complex description. Added concise summary. Restructured description to: (1) clarify it's a long-running operation, (2) explain the problem it solves (unjoined/incorrectly joined events), (3) describe the benefit (improved training/serving), and (4) specify return type. This improves AI agent interpretability by clearly stating when to use this operation and what to expect.

data.summary_suggestion: Write a single user event to event store
data.description_suggestion: Writes a single user event to the event store. The user event captures metadata about how end users interact with the catalog, including event type, timestamp, user information, and item details. This operation is used for real-time event ingestion during user sessions. Returns the written user event object with server-assigned identifiers and timestamps.
